task: I need to get ready for a school project this week, and I’m trying to keep track of what to do first, what to bring, and what can wait until later. Can you help me organize it in a way that would be easy to use while I’m working?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple, easy-to-use project plan you can follow while you work.

## School Project Plan

### 1) Do first
- Read the project directions
- Figure out the due date and requirements
- Make a quick list of what needs to be finished
- Gather the main materials you’ll need

### 2) Next
- Start the hardest part first
- Do the part that takes the longest
- Write down any questions you still have
- Check if anything needs to be done at home, at school, or both

### 3) Bring with you
- Project directions
- Notebook or paper
- Pencil/pen
- Folder or binder
- Any materials for the project
- Charger, if you need your device
- Anything your teacher said to bring

### 4) Can wait until later
- Final decorations
- Extra neat copying
- Optional extra details
- Re-checking things once the main work is done

## Simple work checklist
- [ ] Read directions
- [ ] Gather materials
- [ ] Start main work
- [ ] Finish the important parts
- [ ] Pack what I need
- [ ] Save the final finishing touches for later

If you want, I can also turn this into a **step-by-step plan for each day this week** or a **very short checklist version** you can keep on your phone.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this works. It’s clear and easy to follow.

The checklist part is the best. I can actually use that while I’m working, and the “do first / next / bring / later” setup makes sense.

A day-by-day version might still help, but this is good for now.
